title: Inequality
short: A statement that one expression is less than or greater than another.
full:
An inequality compares two expressions with <, ≤, > or ≥ instead of =. Its solution is usually not a few isolated values but a whole range of them, written in interval notation — a round bracket for a strict < or > (endpoint excluded) and a square bracket for ≤ or ≥ (endpoint included).

To solve a polynomial or rational inequality, move everything to one side, find the boundary values (zeros of the numerator and denominator), and test a sample point in each interval they create. A value that makes a denominator zero is always excluded.
formulas: interval: (a, b) excludes ends, [a, b] includes them
